prompt:
tabskit.tsx is the docs site's Tabs/Accordion kit (compound components over context — works fine, ships today, the EXISTING BEHAVIOR block in run_tests.mjs is green). The accessibility audit and the router team both filed against it, so one ticket, two asks. This layer is server-rendered markup only — keyboard/click handlers are a separate client ticket — what I need is the attribute contract and the prop protocol.

1) WAI-ARIA wiring.
Tabs: the TabList container becomes role="tablist", with aria-label taken from a `label` prop on TabList (omit the attribute when no label). Each Tab keeps its classes and type="button" and gains role="tab", id="<id>-tab-<i>", aria-controls="<id>-panel-<i>", aria-selected="true"/"false" (string, on every tab), and the roving-tabindex pattern: tabindex 0 on the active tab, -1 on the rest. Each TabPanel keeps class and hidden-when-inactive and gains role="tabpanel", id="<id>-panel-<i>", aria-labelledby="<id>-tab-<i>", and tabindex="0" (panels are focus targets). <id> comes from a new `id` prop on Tabs, default "tabs"; <i> is the child's position, same indexing the kit already uses.
Accordion: each item's trigger button keeps class/type and gains id="<id>-trigger-<i>", aria-expanded="true"/"false", aria-controls="<id>-panel-<i>". Each item's panel keeps class and hidden-when-collapsed and gains role="region", id="<id>-panel-<i>", aria-labelledby="<id>-trigger-<i>". `id` prop on Accordion, default "accordion".

2) Controlled/uncontrolled.
Today both widgets only take defaults (defaultIndex / defaultOpen) and own their state. The router integration needs to drive them: Tabs accepts `index?: number` — when provided (0 included) it wins outright and defaultIndex is ignored; when absent, defaultIndex (default 0) seeds internal state exactly as today. Accordion accepts `open?: number[]` with the same rule — `open` provided (empty array included!) means controlled, defaultOpen ignored; several panels open at once stays supported in both modes.

Don't restructure the kit for fun — same exports, same class names, same element nesting; the existing tests pin those and must stay green alongside the new ones. Acceptance is `node run_tests.mjs` fully green (the harness is read-only, bundles tabskit.tsx with the vendored React 19.2.7 in ./vendor/node_modules via the esbuild binary on PATH, and compares renderToStaticMarkup output with attribute order/name-case normalized). Offline — the vendor tree is already there, no npm anything. No effects/refs/browser APIs; plain TS annotations.
notes: react 19.2.7 + react-dom 19.2.7 + scheduler 0.27.0 (production cjs builds, complete packages minus READMEs) vendored in files/vendor/node_modules. Feature seed: baseline tabskit.tsx works and passes the EXISTING BEHAVIOR block; the feature blocks fail until ARIA attributes + controlled props are added. Harness bundles via esbuild on PATH, offline. Bundle output .bundle_out/ created and removed by the harness.
